Simulate AKAP79 deterministically and add noise

simAKAP79.Rmd
library(errors)
library(uqsa)

This article provides code to simulate the AKAP79 model (one time, no sampling) also considering measurement noise. We are plotting the model with default parameters which are not expected to fit the data (this is the starting point).

Load the Model

This model is included with the package. To load your own model, see the article “Build and simulate your own Model”.

f <- uqsa_example("AKAP79",full.names=TRUE)
m <- model_from_tsv(f)
o <- as_ode(m)
#> Loading required namespace: pracma
c_path(o) <- write_c_code(generate_code(o))
so_path(o) <- shlib(o)
print(o)
#>                 Model name : AKAP79
#>                     C file : /tmp/RtmpiY0iWI/RtmpQMQrCy/RtmpWsZ5fX/b316367e5f817645/AKAP79.c [2026-06-26 14:39:00.87736]
#>             shared library : /tmp/RtmpiY0iWI/RtmpQMQrCy/RtmpWsZ5fX/b316367e5f817645/AKAP79.so [2026-06-26 14:39:00.87736]
#>  Number of state variables : 11
#>       Number of parameters : 33
#>          Number of outputs : 1
#>          Conservation laws : 5
#>            Transformations : yes

Conservation laws (determined by as_ode):

print(o$conservationLaws)
#>       Eliminates value Constant         ConstantName
#> AKAR4         14  0.20     0.20 AKAR4_ConservedConst
#> CaN           11  1.50     1.50   CaN_ConservedConst
#> Rii_C          4  0.63     0.63 Rii_C_ConservedConst
#> cAMP           2  0.00     0.00  cAMP_ConservedConst
#> Rii            1  6.30     6.30   Rii_ConservedConst
#>                                                                                    Formula
#> AKAR4                                             AKAR4_ConservedConst - (+AKAR4_C+AKAR4p)
#> CaN                                         CaN_ConservedConst - (+RiiP_CaN+RiiP_cAMP_CaN)
#> Rii_C                    Rii_C_ConservedConst - (+RiiP_C+RiiP_C_cAMP+C+Rii_C_cAMP+AKAR4_C)
#> cAMP      cAMP_ConservedConst - (+RiiP_cAMP+RiiP_C_cAMP+Rii_cAMP+Rii_C_cAMP+RiiP_cAMP_CaN)
#> Rii   Rii_ConservedConst - (+RiiP+RiiP_cAMP-1*C+Rii_cAMP+RiiP_CaN+RiiP_cAMP_CaN-1*AKAR4_C)

With conservation laws, some species are calculated algebraically. Their initial values are turned into input parameters (using the found law):

With this hypothetical relationship (\(c\) is a constant):

\[A+B = c\,,\]

we can determine that

\[c = A_0 + B_0\,.\]

And thus we can replac either of the two species:

\[A(t) = A_0 + B_0 - B(t)\]

And \(A_0+B_0\) are turned into an input called A_ConservedConst (the \(c\) above) with the value determined from the stated initial condition.

Load Experiments (data)

This also includes instructions for the simulator.

ex <- experiments(m)
print(m)
#> $cAMP_increase
#>     time transformation dose
#> tr1  610       SET_cAMP  0.1
#> tr2  810       SET_cAMP  0.2
#> tr3 1010       SET_cAMP  0.5
#> tr4 1210       SET_cAMP  1.0
#> tr5 1410       SET_cAMP  2.0
#> 
#> $Compound
#>               value Unit     uniprot
#> Rii            6.30   µM  KAP2_HUMAN
#> cAMP           0.00   µM        NONE
#> RiiP           0.00   µM        NONE
#> Rii_C          0.63   µM        NONE
#> RiiP_cAMP      0.00   µM        NONE
#> RiiP_C         0.00   µM        NONE
#> RiiP_C_cAMP    0.00   µM        NONE
#> C              0.00   µM KAPCB_HUMAN
#> Rii_cAMP       0.00   µM        NONE
#> Rii_C_cAMP     0.00   µM        NONE
#> CaN            1.50   µM PP2BA_HUMAN
#> RiiP_CaN       0.00   µM        NONE
#> RiiP_cAMP_CaN  0.00   µM        NONE
#> AKAR4          0.20   µM        NONE
#> AKAR4_C        0.00   µM        NONE
#> AKAR4p         0.00   µM        NONE
#>                                                        Comment
#> Rii           also KAP3_HUMAN because Rii = RII_alpha+RII_beta
#> cAMP                                                      NONE
#> RiiP                                            phosphorylated
#> Rii_C                                                     NONE
#> RiiP_cAMP                                                 NONE
#> RiiP_C                                                    NONE
#> RiiP_C_cAMP                                               NONE
#> C                                                         NONE
#> Rii_cAMP                                                  NONE

#> CaN                                                PP3R1_HUMAN
#> RiiP_CaN                                                  NONE

#> AKAR4                                       RRID:addgene_61619

#>                                                                formula
#> k3p_7                         b_AKAP*AKAPon_1 + (1 - b_AKAP)*AKAPoff_1
#> k4p_4                         b_AKAP*AKAPon_3 + (1 - b_AKAP)*AKAPoff_3
#> k4p_8                         b_AKAP*AKAPon_1 + (1 - b_AKAP)*AKAPoff_1
#> k3p_3                         b_AKAP*AKAPon_3 + (1 - b_AKAP)*AKAPoff_3
#> k4_4p b_AKAP*(k4p_4 + k3p_7)/kmON + (1 - b_AKAP)*(k4p_4 + k3p_7)/kmOFF
#> k3_3p b_AKAP*(k3p_3 + k4p_8)/kmON + (1 - b_AKAP)*(k3p_3 + k4p_8)/kmOFF
#> k2_1                                                       k1_2 * KD_T
#>                      unit
#> k3p_7                 1/s
#> k4p_4                 1/s
#> k4p_8                 1/s
#> k3p_3                 1/s
#> k4_4p 1/(micromolarity*s)
#> k3_3p 1/(micromolarity*s)
#> k2_1                  1/s
#> 
#> $Input
#>        value unit
#> b_AKAP     0    1
#> 
#> $Output
#>                        formula unit
#> AKAR4pOUT (AKAR4p*5)*71.67+100   µM
#> 
#> $Parameter
#>                   value median   stdv  prior.distribution scale   unit
#> k5_1        1.667836985  1.519 3.0000 Normal distribution log10   s^-1
#> k1_2        0.094637887 -0.306 3.0000 Normal distribution log10 1/µM*s
#> k3_2       -2.483544729 -2.264 3.0000 Normal distribution log10 1/µM*s
#> k2_3       -0.230730988 -1.807 3.0000 Normal distribution log10   s^-1
#> k3_4       -1.136198159 -2.796 3.0000 Normal distribution log10   s^-1
#> k4_3       -2.147382382 -1.824 3.0000 Normal distribution log10 1/µM*s
#> k4_1       -2.284184910 -1.420 3.0000 Normal distribution log10 1/µM*s
#> k1_4       -2.928144261 -2.585 3.0000 Normal distribution log10   s^-1
#> k8_7       -2.149448876 -1.824 3.0000 Normal distribution log10 1/µM*s
#> k7_8       -1.079309999 -2.796 3.0000 Normal distribution log10   s^-1
#> k5_6       -0.177019247 -0.305 3.0000 Normal distribution log10 1/µM*s
#> k6_5       -0.225914640  0.150 3.0000 Normal distribution log10   s^-1
#> k8_5       -0.998736089  0.322 3.0000 Normal distribution log10 1/µM*s
#> k7_6       -1.009160401 -0.525 3.0000 Normal distribution log10 1/µM*s
#> k6_7       -1.653376958 -1.745 3.0000 Normal distribution log10   s^-1
#> k6_2        1.529394655  1.519 3.0000 Normal distribution log10   s^-1
#> k5_8       -3.696929487 -3.523 3.0000 Normal distribution log10   s^-1
#> AKAPoff_1  -0.531584138  0.415 3.0000 Normal distribution log10   s^-1
#> AKAPoff_3   1.173671580  1.301 3.0000 Normal distribution log10   s^-1
#> AKAPon_1   -0.365851062 -0.347 0.2790 Normal distribution log10   s^-1
#> AKAPon_3    0.236153457  0.301 3.0000 Normal distribution log10   s^-1
#> kf_C_AKAR4 -1.742480407 -1.745 0.0969 Normal distribution log10 1/µM*s
#> kb_C_AKAR4 -0.980459545 -0.975 0.0969 Normal distribution log10   s^-1
#> kcat_AKARp  1.007798230  1.009 0.0969 Normal distribution log10   s^-1
#> kmOFF       2.009602466  2.000 0.1760 Normal distribution log10     µM
#> kmON       -0.005703976  0.000 0.1760 Normal distribution log10     µM
#> KD_T       -0.175252976 -0.155 0.3010 Normal distribution log10     µM
#> 
#> $Reaction
#>                                   kinetic.law       reactants arw
#> r51                                k5_1*Rii_C           Rii_C <=>
#> r14                 k4_1*RiiP*C - k1_4*RiiP_C        RiiP + C <=>
#> r12       k1_2*RiiP_C*cAMP - k2_1*RiiP_C_cAMP   RiiP_C + cAMP <=>
#> r43           k4_3*cAMP*RiiP - k3_4*RiiP_cAMP     cAMP + RiiP <=>
#> r23       k3_2*RiiP_cAMP*C - k2_3*RiiP_C_cAMP   RiiP_cAMP + C <=>
#> r78             k8_7*cAMP*Rii - k7_8*Rii_cAMP      cAMP + Rii <=>
#> r56         k5_6*Rii_C*cAMP - k6_5*Rii_C_cAMP    Rii_C + cAMP <=>
#> r76         k7_6*Rii_cAMP*C - k6_7*Rii_C_cAMP    Rii_cAMP + C <=>
#> r62                           k6_2*Rii_C_cAMP      Rii_C_cAMP <=>
#> r58                   k8_5*Rii*C - k5_8*Rii_C         Rii + C <=>
#> r44           k4_4p*RiiP*CaN - k4p_4*RiiP_CaN      RiiP + CaN <=>
#> r33 k3_3p*CaN*RiiP_cAMP - k3p_3*RiiP_cAMP_CaN CaN + RiiP_cAMP <=>
#> r48                            k4p_8*RiiP_CaN        RiiP_CaN <=>
#> r37                       k3p_7*RiiP_cAMP_CaN   RiiP_cAMP_CaN <=>
#> r1    kf_C_AKAR4*C*AKAR4 - kb_C_AKAR4*AKAR4_C       C + AKAR4 <=>
#> r2                         kcat_AKARp*AKAR4_C         AKAR4_C <=>
#>           products is.reversible
#> r51         RiiP_C             0
#> r14         RiiP_C             1
#> r12    RiiP_C_cAMP             1
#> r43      RiiP_cAMP             1
#> r23    RiiP_C_cAMP             1
#> r78       Rii_cAMP             1
#> r56     Rii_C_cAMP             1
#> r76     Rii_C_cAMP             1
#> r62    RiiP_C_cAMP             0
#> r58          Rii_C             1
#> r44       RiiP_CaN             1
#> r33  RiiP_cAMP_CaN             1
#> r48      Rii + CaN             0
#> r37 CaN + Rii_cAMP             0
#> r1         AKAR4_C             1
#> r2      AKAR4p + C             0
#> 
#> $Transformation
#>          cAMP
#> SET_cAMP dose

Simulate

This will make a function s, which will always simulate the scenarios described in the experiments list, but for user supplied parameters.

s <- simulator.c(ex,o,parMap=log10ParMap,omit=2)
p <- values(m$Parameter)
y <- s(p)

Plot 


e.g. <- 10 # which experiment to plot
tm <- ex[[e.g.]]$outputTime

par(bty='n',xaxp=c(80,120,4))
plot(
    as.errors(tm),
    ex[[e.g.]]$data[1,],
    type='l',
    ylim=c(90,130), ylab="AKAR4p",
    xlab="t",
    main=sprintf("Experiment %s",names(ex)[e.g.])
)
lines(tm,y[[e.g.]]$func[1,,1],lwd=1.5,col="purple")
